name: Running Statistics
description: Calculates rolling min, max, mean, and standard deviation
author: NISystem

window_size.description: Number of samples for rolling window

code:
# Running Statistics Calculator
# Calculates rolling statistics over a configurable window

import math

input_tag = config.get('input_tag', 'Temperature_1')
window = config.get('window_size', 60)

value = tags.get(input_tag, 0)

# Initialize buffer
if 'buffer' not in state:
    state['buffer'] = []

# Add value to buffer
state['buffer'].append(value)

# Keep only window samples
if len(state['buffer']) > window:
    state['buffer'] = state['buffer'][-window:]

buf = state['buffer']
n = len(buf)

# Calculate statistics
min_val = min(buf)
max_val = max(buf)
mean_val = sum(buf) / n

# Standard deviation
if n > 1:
    variance = sum((x - mean_val) ** 2 for x in buf) / (n - 1)
    std_dev = math.sqrt(variance)
else:
    std_dev = 0

publish(f'py.{input_tag}_Min', min_val)
publish(f'py.{input_tag}_Max', max_val)
publish(f'py.{input_tag}_Mean', mean_val)
publish(f'py.{input_tag}_StdDev', std_dev)
